officialCodexDocs.usedFor: Codex config precedence, user config, project .codex/config.toml, and trust boundary.
Profile file naming and profile overlay behavior.
Project config restrictions for provider, auth, profile, notification, and telemetry keys.
Custom agent TOML location, required keys, inheritance, and optional fields.
Parallel subagent workflow boundaries and delegation tradeoffs.
Skill folder shape, discovery roots, progressive disclosure, and skills.config disable entries.
Global and project instruction discovery via AGENTS.md.
Credential cache handling and why auth.json is local-only.

repositoryWorkflowNucleus.summary: Use the OMP workflow-kit repo operating model as the harness nucleus workflow. Translate it into each harness's native instruction, config, agent, and skill language instead of copying live OMP runtime state.
portablePolicy: Keep a global layer for default workflow instructions, sticky safety rules, and reusable skills.
Keep a project layer for repo-local instructions, repo config, issue-tracker guidance, triage labels, domain guidance, scope ledger, handoffs, and project skills.
Use an idempotent apply/check model where a marker manifest defines what applied means and existing files are not overwritten.
Preserve the full workflow trace from grilled plan to PRD, issues, triage, one issue/worktree/PR implementation, verification evidence, and handoff when blocked.
Require project skills to live one directory below nucleus/skills with SKILL.md frontmatter and concrete Use when triggers.
Treat GitHub issue/PR templates and workflow labels as repo workflow surfaces when the repo uses GitHub.
codexTranslation: Project-specific workflow policy belongs in repo-local AGENTS.md/.codex/config candidates and .agents/skills where it is useful across harnesses.

General reusable workflow policy belongs in user-level instruction or skill surfaces such as ~/.agents/skills; direct OMP bundled-agent role ports are superseded by the shared nucleus per-agent package contract.
Codex custom agents are optional harness adapters only; the canonical target model is one Vercel-shaped package per shared nucleus agent with canonical names and no harness prefixes.
Future renderers must produce dry-run manifests before writing live ~/.codex or repo config.

templateBoundaries.boundary: Tracked base templates may describe safe discovery and feature toggles only. They must not set default model/provider, auth, telemetry, notifications, trusted-project state, or machine-specific paths.
Profile templates are optional user-level overlays selected by --profile. They may tune review effort or sandbox policy, but must not alter provider routing or credentials.

Historical OMP-prefixed custom agent TOML files are validation fixtures only. Active renderers must not emit omp-designer, omp-planner, omp-reviewer, omp-librarian, or other harness-prefixed agents; future native agents must be generated from the shared nucleus per-agent package contract using canonical names.
Skill enable/disable templates may show explicit path toggles only. They must not delete duplicate skills, vendor plugin caches, or copy private global skill contents.

ompAgentMappings.rationale: Direct OMP designer role ports are superseded. Future UI/UX workflow activation must render the shared spidertron package, using the canonical name without an omp- prefix.
Codex already has an explorer role for read-heavy codebase scouting; keep the native role unless a later review needs OMP-specific output formatting.
Direct OMP librarian role ports are superseded. Future source-verified research activation must render the shared science-pack package, using the canonical name without an omp- prefix.
Low-reasoning mechanical worker maps to Codex native worker; no omp-specific role file is needed.

Direct OMP planner role ports are superseded. Future architecture/planning activation must render the relevant shared nucleus packages such as blueprint, ghosts, roboports, and main-bus, using canonical names without an omp- prefix.
Specialized test-authoring agent with contract-defending discipline; worth adapting as a nucleus-backed test specialist.

Direct OMP reviewer role ports are superseded. Future review activation must render the relevant shared nucleus packages such as biters, spitters, bus-first, and lab, using canonical names without an omp- prefix.
General-purpose delegated execution is already the Codex worker/default surface; a direct port would duplicate native behavior.

skillCandidateMappings.rationale: Port only the bounded side-question workflow, not OMP's ephemeral runtime subthread.
Port the goal interview workflow; enabling persistent goal runtime state remains host-native.
Port the handoff-writing discipline, not automatic OMP session spawning.
Port complaint-to-rule drafting as a reviewable workflow; do not install generated rules automatically.
Port plan-before-execute guidance where a custom planning agent is too heavy.
Port tangential-work delegation prompts; do not claim to launch OMP background agents.

localOnlyCodexSurfaces.reason: Credential cache and refresh tokens; official auth docs require treating it as password-like local material.
Private conversation transcripts and runtime state.
Private session index and history metadata.
Temporary plugin sync, cache, and staging state.
Downloaded plugin cache and marketplace artifacts.
Generated blobs or binary state if present.
User-provided attachments and pasted text metadata.
App/server state, reconciliation state, and private UI state.
User-specific schedules, monitors, and run state.
Browser integration configuration and runtime state.
Desktop-control app state and machine-specific binaries/config.
Private memory store and rollout summaries.

generatedCandidateSurfaces.notes: May be rendered into a temp directory for validation; do not overwrite the project or user live file in this issue.
User-level profile candidate only after explicit approval; do not change Codex default provider/model.

Future renderer target: one Vercel-shaped package per canonical shared nucleus agent, using canonical names and no harness prefixes. OMP-prefixed Codex custom-agent templates are superseded context only.
Enable/disable examples must be merged by a future renderer and must never remove duplicate skill directories.

dryRunValidationStrategy: Render active templates into a temporary directory, never directly into ~/.codex; superseded OMP-prefixed custom-agent templates are not active renderer inputs.
Parse every rendered TOML template with the vendored in-process TOML parser.
Compare OMP agent mapping rows against distributions/snapshots/omp-builtins/source.json and fail when a bundled agent is unmapped.
Validate that the Codex adapter preserves the workflow-kit repo lifecycle as portable policy rather than copying live OMP runtime files.
Reject rendered files containing absolute private home paths, API key/token-looking text, auth cache names as destinations, provider routing keys, or model default changes in the base template.
Validate that official Codex doc references cover config, profiles, custom agents/subagents, skills, AGENTS.md, and auth/local credential boundaries.

Print a dry-run manifest showing active candidate destination paths, required human approvals, and skipped local-only surfaces; the manifest must not list OMP-prefixed custom agents as shared agent activation targets.
Future agent activation must render per-agent Vercel-shaped packages from nucleus/agents/shared-nucleus-agents.json with canonical names and no harness prefixes.
Require a separate future issue/PR to perform any write into ~/.codex, .codex/agents, or .agents/skills.

humanDecisionResolutions.question: What replaces adapted OMP agents as Codex activation targets?
humanDecisionResolutions.resolution: Direct OMP-prefixed Codex custom agents are superseded. Future agent activation targets the shared nucleus contract: one Vercel-shaped package per canonical agent name, with no harness prefix.

humanDecisionResolutions.question: Should designer, librarian, planner, and reviewer OMP role candidates remain active renderer targets?
humanDecisionResolutions.resolution: No. Keep their rationale only as superseded adapter-plan context; active renderers must not emit omp-designer, omp-librarian, omp-planner, or omp-reviewer.

humanDecisionResolutions.question: Which skill candidates should be repo-local nucleus/skills versus personal ~/.agents/skills?
humanDecisionResolutions.resolution: Use the OMP workflow-kit split: repo workflow and domain skills go in nucleus/skills; general reusable workflow skills go in ~/.agents/skills; inspect the active OMP skill roots before final placement.

humanDecisionResolutions.question: Should the optional omp-harness profile set sandbox/approval defaults, or should it only document recommended CLI flags?
humanDecisionResolutions.resolution: The optional omp-harness profile should set sandbox and approval defaults.

humanDecisionResolutions.question: What review/approval policy is required before merging any generated template into live ~/.codex/config.toml?
humanDecisionResolutions.resolution: Default to a strict manual gate: separate issue/PR, dry-run rendered diff, dangerous-key validation, backup of the live file, and explicit human approval before any write.

liveConfigApprovalPolicyOptions.policy: Require a separate issue/PR, dry-run diff, validation pass, live-file backup, and explicit human approval before writing ~/.codex/config.toml.
Allow a local apply command only after the dry-run checker passes and creates a backup; still forbid provider, auth, trust, telemetry, and default-model changes.
Never write live config from the harness; render instructions and let the human manually copy reviewed entries.
